findings.description: The exact same SQL SELECT query for messages (lines 39-44) exists verbatim in hooks/useMessages.ts (lines 17-22). This is a 7-line SQL string duplication. If the schema changes, both copies must be updated.
findings.current_code:
const stmt = db.prepare(`
  SELECT id, session_id, role, content, tool_calls, tool_name, timestamp, token_count, active
  FROM messages
  WHERE session_id = $sid AND active = 1
  ORDER BY timestamp ASC
`)
findings.suggested_change: Extract the SQL query to a shared constant, e.g. src/utils/queries.ts, and import it in both locations.

findings.description: The while-loop iteration over sql.js Statement rows (while stmt.step() / stmt.getAsObject()) appears in both App.tsx (lines 46-48) and useMessages.ts (lines 25-39). Different detail level but same structural pattern.
findings.suggested_change: Create a shared utility function like `queryAll(db, sql, params): Message[]` that wraps prepare/bind/step/getAsObject/free.

findings.description: The regex for sanitizing session names to filenames appears in ChatView.tsx (line 24) and almost identically in export.ts (line 44 — same regex plus .slice(0,50)). Duplicate logic that could diverge.
findings.current_code: `${(session.title || session.id).replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, '_')}.md`
findings.suggested_change: Extract to a shared utility function like `sanitizeFilename(name: string): string` in utils/format.ts or utils/export.ts.

findings.description: handleDragOver and handleDrop handlers both contain identical e.preventDefault() and e.stopPropagation() calls (2 lines × 2 handlers = 4 lines duplicated).
findings.suggested_change: Create a shared wrapper: `const prevent = (e: Event) => { e.preventDefault(); e.stopPropagation(); }` and call it from both handlers.

findings.description: The positional array-to-Session object mapping (lines 62-74) mirrors the column order from the SQL query above it. This is fragile — adding a column requires updating both the SELECT and all the positional indices. A similar pattern exists in useMessages.ts (lines 28-38) with field-by-field construction.
findings.current_code:
const sessions = rows[0]?.values.map((row: any[]) => ({
  id: row[0] as string,
  title: row[1] as string | null,
  source: row[2] as string,
  model: row[3] as string,
  started_at: row[4] as number,
  ended_at: row[5] as number | null,
  message_count: row[6] as number,
  tool_call_count: row[7] as number,
  input_tokens: row[8] as number,
  output_tokens: row[9] as number,
  archived: row[10] as number,
}))
findings.suggested_change: Use db.exec() with column-name access or create a mapping helper that derives keys from query columns. Alternatively, use a single shared function that takes columns+rows and produces typed objects.

findings.description: The catch-block pattern `setError(err.message || 'Failed to ...')` followed by `setLoading(false)` in finally appears identically in useSqlite.ts (lines 77-80) and similar forms in useMessages.ts (lines 43-46) and the first try-catch in useSqlite.ts (lines 25-28). Three occurrences of essentially the same 3-line error handling pattern.
findings.suggested_change: Create a generic error-handling wrapper: `async function withErrorHandling<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, onError: string): Promise<T | undefined>` or similar pattern.

findings.description: The manual field-by-field mapping from getAsObject() result to typed Message object (lines 28-38) is redundant — getAsObject() already returns an object with the same keys. The only mismatch is that getAsObject returns { [key: string]: any }, so a simple type cast would suffice.
findings.current_code:
messages.push({
  id: row.id,
  session_id: row.session_id,
  role: row.role,
  content: row.content,
  tool_calls: row.tool_calls,
  tool_name: row.tool_name,
  timestamp: row.timestamp,
  token_count: row.token_count,
  active: row.active,
})
findings.suggested_change: Replace with `messages.push(row as Message)` since column names match exactly.

findings.description: The db field is typed as `any | null` instead of using the proper sql.js Database type which is already declared in src/sql.js.d.ts.
findings.current_code: db: any | null
findings.suggested_change: Import Database from sql.js and type as `Database | null`.

findings.description: Module-level `SQL` variable typed as `any`, losing all type safety from the sql.js type declarations.
findings.current_code: let SQL: any = null
findings.suggested_change: Type as `SqlJsStatic | null` (importing SqlJsStatic from the sql.js module declaration).

findings.description: Database instance typed as `any` instead of the available `Database` type from sql.js declarations.
findings.current_code: const db: any = new SQL.Database(uint8)
findings.suggested_change: Type as `Database` from sql.js.

findings.description: Variable named `rewinds` but it's just `session.tool_call_count` with a comment '// approx'. This is misleading — it's not an approximation, it's the exact tool_call_count. The concept of 'rewinds' is not defined anywhere.
findings.current_code: const rewinds = session.tool_call_count // approx
findings.suggested_change: Remove the variable entirely and use `session.tool_call_count` directly in the JSX, or rename to a clearer concept like `toolCalls`.

findings.description: Store setter actions use inconsistent abbreviated single-letter parameter names: setLoading(v), setError(e), setSourceFilter(f), setSortMode(m). This hurts readability compared to full-word names.
findings.current_code:
setLoading: (v: boolean) => void
setError: (e: string | null) => void
setSourceFilter: (f: string) => void
setSortMode: (m: SortMode) => void
findings.suggested_change: Use full parameter names: (loading: boolean), (error: string | null), (source: string), (mode: SortMode).

findings.description: `useSessions` hook is exported but never imported by any component in the entire project. Only `useSources` from the same file is imported (in FilterBar.tsx). The function creates redundant useMemo wrapping of getFilteredSessions which is already cached by zustand.
findings.suggested_change: Either remove `useSessions` (dead code) or make it the single source of truth for session filtering and remove the duplicate useMemo in SessionList.tsx.

findings.description: The `truncateTitle` function is exported from format.ts but is never imported or used anywhere in the project.
findings.current_code: export function truncateTitle(content: string, max = 80): string {
findings.suggested_change: Remove the function if not needed, or add a comment documenting its intended use.

findings.description: A deeply nested ternary expression inside a template literal className string. The outer ternary (isUser ? ... : ...) has inner ternaries/operations that are hard to parse at a glance.
findings.suggested_change: Extract className computation to a variable above the JSX return: `const bubbleClass = isUser ? 'border-2 border-white/20 bg-white/5 text-white p-4' : 'border-2 border-white/10 bg-white/[0.03] text-white/90 p-4'`

findings.description: Hardcoded number 200 for content truncation limit. No named constant explaining what this represents.
findings.current_code: content.length > 200 ? content.slice(0, 200) + ' [ … ]' : content
findings.suggested_change: Extract to a named constant: `const TOOL_CONTENT_PREVIEW_LENGTH = 200`

findings.description: Hardcoded number 50 for filename truncation limit. No named constant.
findings.current_code: const safeName = (session.title || session.id).replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, '_').slice(0, 50)
findings.suggested_change: Extract to a named constant: `const MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH = 50`

findings.description: Hardcoded debounce timeout of 300ms. No named constant.
findings.suggested_change: Extract to a named constant: `const DEBOUNCE_MS = 300`

findings.description: useMessages hook both loads messages (data fetching) AND manages cache eviction via the cleanup useEffect. It directly calls store actions (setError, clearMessagesCache) instead of exposing an error state. The hook is over 65 lines for what could be a simpler pattern.
findings.suggested_change: Separate data-loading concerns from cache management. Consider extracting the cache cleanup to the component that uses it. Return { messages, loading, error } instead of mutating store error.

findings.description: getFilteredSessions is a 37-line function (lines 67-104) with three sequential filter steps, a sort switch, and an inner function. Encapsulated in a zustand store as a 'derived' getter but evaluated as a regular function call, which means it recalculates on every render.
findings.suggested_change: Keep filtering logic out of the store entirely. Move to a custom hook (useFilteredSessions) that memoizes with useMemo and proper dependencies.

findings.description: getStats iterates all filtered sessions to compute totals every time it's called. With the zustart selector pattern `useStore(s => s.getStats)()`, this runs on every store change, not just when stats change.
findings.suggested_change: Compute stats as a derived value with selectors or use a memoized selector outside the store. Alternatively, use zustand's `subscribeWithSelector` to compute only when relevant data changes.

findings.description: loadMessagesForExport is defined inside handleExportAllMd (lines 37-50), so it's recreated on every render of App. It also duplicates the same SQL query pattern from useMessages. This mixes data access with UI logic.
findings.suggested_change: Move loadMessagesForExport out of the component (to utils/ or export.ts) and memoize handleExportAllMd with useCallback. Better yet, have exportAllMd take the db directly.

findings.description: SessionList wraps getFilteredSessions() in useMemo, but getFilteredSessions itself is recreated each time the store changes because it's a store function reference that changes on every store update. The useMemo here is essentially a no-op.
findings.current_code: const sessions = useMemo(() => getFilteredSessions(), [getFilteredSessions])
findings.suggested_change: Use zustand selector pattern to only select the derived value: `const sessions = useStore(s => s.getFilteredSessions())` or refactor filtering as a proper memoized selector outside the store.

findings.description: Most store setters are simple `set({ prop })` one-liners, but `clearMessagesCache` (lines 54-58) manually destructures and rebuilds the cache object. Also `cacheMessages` (line 52-53) uses an updater function while the simple setters use direct value. Two different patterns in the same store.
findings.suggested_change: Keep all store mutations using the updater pattern `set(state => ...)` consistently, or all using direct values. The destructuring in clearMessagesCache is clever but harder to read than a simple delete + spread.

findings.description: Uses `(e.target as HTMLInputElement).value` instead of properly typed React event generics. This pattern appears in 5 places across 3 files (DropZone.tsx line 43, SearchBar.tsx line 26, FilterBar.tsx lines 21/30/42). Some code uses `e.target as HTMLInputElement` while others (like BracketButton) properly extend HTMLButtonElement attributes.
findings.current_code: onChange={(e) => setSourceFilter((e.target as HTMLSelectElement).value)}
findings.suggested_change: Use properly typed React event generics: `onChange={(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLSelectElement>) => setSourceFilter(e.target.value)}`

findings.description: Transition duration values are inconsistent: DropZone uses `duration-200`, SessionCard uses `duration-100`, BracketButton uses `duration-100`. Without documented reason for the difference, this looks like inconsistency.
findings.current_code:
DropZone: 'transition-colors duration-200'
SessionCard: 'transition-colors duration-100'
BracketButton: 'transition-colors duration-100'
findings.suggested_change: Standardize to a single duration value across all interactive elements (e.g. `duration-100` for hover effects, `duration-200` for larger transitions).

findings.description: Most filter/UI initialState values are primitives (false, '', 'newest'), but `sourceFilter` is initialized to the string `'all'` which is a sentinel value meaning 'no filter'. This mixes two different patterns for 'no filter': boolean toggle (showArchived: false) vs sentinel string (sourceFilter: 'all').
findings.current_code:
showArchived: false,
sourceFilter: 'all',
findings.suggested_change: Consider making sourceFilter nullable (`null | string`) where null means 'no filter', or consistently use sentinel values with clear naming.

findings.description: The comment '// approx' on the rewinds variable is misleading — the value is not approximate, it's the exact tool_call_count. If it's truly approximate, the calculation is wrong. If it's exact, the comment is wrong.
findings.current_code: const rewinds = session.tool_call_count // approx
findings.suggested_change: Remove the comment if `rewinds === tool_call_count`. If rewinds truly differs from tool calls, update the logic to compute the correct value.

findings.description: The CDN URL for sql.js WASM is hardcoded with no justification or alternative. No comment explaining why it uses a CDN (not local bundle), what version it pins to, or how to configure for offline use.
findings.current_code: locateFile: (file: string) => `https://sql.js.org/dist/${file}`,
findings.suggested_change: Add a comment: '// CDN fallback for sql.js WASM — update the URL for self-hosted deployments' or document the reason.

findings.description: Comments like `{/* Top bar */}`, `{/* Main content */}`, `{/* Sidebar - session list */}` describe what the code does (obvious from structure) rather than why decisions were made. These are visual separators that could be replaced with cleaner component decomposition.
findings.suggested_change: Consider extracting top bar, sidebar, and main content into named subcomponents instead of using 'what' comments. The comments are a code smell for missing component boundaries.

findings.description: The cleanup useEffect in useMessages clears the entire message cache for a session when the component unmounts. This forces a full reload if the user navigates back, which is unnecessary for most navigation patterns.
findings.suggested_change: Remove the cache-eviction cleanup and let the cache persist across navigation. Only evict on explicit refresh or when memory pressure is detected.

findings.description: useMessages uses local state for loading (localLoading useState) but global store state for error (setError). This is inconsistent — one concern (data loading) spans two state management approaches. DropZone also mixes local (wasmError) and global (error from useStore) error states.
findings.suggested_change: Choose one pattern: either return { messages, loading, error } from the hook (fully local state) or use store for both loading and error (fully global). The mixed pattern is confusing.

findings.description: The wrapping div className `flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-screen bg-black text-white font-mono` appears twice: once for the loading state (line 49) and once for the drop zone (line 57) with only a `p-8` difference. ~70% duplicated styling.
findings.suggested_change: Extract shared wrapper into a single container that conditionally renders loading vs drop content, avoiding duplicate layout classes.

summary: Scanned all 22 source files. Found 33 findings across 5 categories. Top issues: (1) Duplicate SQL query string in App.tsx and useMessages.ts — the most critical as schema changes require two edits. (2) 14 instances of `any` type usage defeats TypeScript's purpose. (3) Dead code: `useSessions` hook exported but never imported; `truncateTitle` never used. (4) Misleading 'rewinds' variable. (5) Redundant field-by-field mapping in useMessages.ts where `getAsObject()` returns matching keys. (6) Mixing local and global state patterns in hooks. (7) No TODO/FIXME/HACK comments anywhere across all files. Overall risk tier: CAREFUL — most changes are safe refactors, but some require coordinated edits across files (extracting shared SQL, error handling wrappers, store refactoring).
naming_suggestions: Type `db: any` → `db: Database | null` (import Database from sql.js)
Remove unused export `useSessions` or rename purpose clearly
Remove unused export `truncateTitle` or add jsdoc explaining intended use
